apt_metadata.depends: libasound2t64 (>= 1.0.17), libatk-bridge2.0-0t64 (>= 2.5.3), libatk1.0-0t64 (>= 2.32.0), libatspi2.0-0t64 (>= 2.9.90), libc6 (>= 2.34), libcairo2 (>= 1.6.0), libcups2t64 (>= 1.7.0), libdbus-1-3 (>= 1.10), libexpat1 (>= 2.1~beta3), libgbm1 (>= 21.1.0), libgcc-s1 (>= 4.2), libglib2.0-0t64 (>= 2.40.0), libgtk-3-0t64 (>= 3.9.10), libnspr4 (>= 2:4.9-2~), libnss3 (>= 2:3.30), libpango-1.0-0 (>= 1.14.0), libstdc++6 (>= 4.1.1), libudev1 (>= 183), libx11-6 (>= 2:1.4.99.1), libxcb1 (>= 1.9.2), libxcomposite1 (>= 1:0.4.6), libxdamage1 (>= 1:1.1), libxext6, libxfixes3, libxkbcommon0 (>= 0.5.0), libxrandr2, nodejs
apt_metadata.description:
open source note taking and to-do application
This package contains a free, open source note taking and to-do application,
which can handle a large number of notes organised into notebooks. The notes
are searchable, can be copied, tagged and modified either from the
applications directly or from your own text editor. The notes are in Markdown
format.
.
Notes exported from Evernote via .enex files can be imported into Joplin,
including the formatted content (which is converted to Markdown), resources
(images, attachments, etc.) and complete metadata (geolocation, updated time,
created time, etc.). Plain Markdown files can also be imported.
.
The notes can be synchronised with various cloud services including Nextcloud,
Dropbox, OneDrive, WebDAV or the file system (for example with a network
directory). When synchronising the notes, notebooks, tags and other metadata
are saved to plain text files which can be easily inspected, backed up and
moved around.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use joplin because ### joplin.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair joplin with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `joplin --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### joplin.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install joplin
Install with: sudo apt install joplin
